task.name: Backend task reporter
task.objective: Compose a complete, evidence-backed task report and submit it to the tmux-cli backend via the task-report MCP tool. The MCP tool hard-rejects missing fields, contentless stubs, and out-of-enum values with NO coercion — this command's job is to derive every field properly BEFORE the wire call so a rejection never happens.

glossary.term: The problem being reported. Resolved from: (1) $ARGUMENTS free-form text, (2) a path in $ARGUMENTS to a saved outbox draft (.tmux-cli/task-reports/outbox-*.md) for retry, (3) the current conversation context when $ARGUMENTS is empty.
Optional. A goal-\d+ token found in PROBLEM or the conversation, used to locate on-disk evidence. Absent for non-goal reports.
.tmux-cli/task-reports/ — where a composed report is saved when the backend is unreachable, so the work is never lost.

llm.mandate: NEVER submit a stub proposed_fix ("fix it", "tbd", "n/a", "none", "not applicable", "to be determined") — the backend rejects them; derive a concrete remediation or name the smallest concrete diagnostic step
NEVER invent evidence — quote verbatim from files/logs or omit the Evidence section line; paraphrased "evidence" is worse than none
NEVER use a category or severity outside the closed sets in step 3 — the backend rejects them with NO coercion
NEVER modify any project file — the only writes allowed are OUTBOX_DIR drafts on submission failure
One report per invocation — never batch multiple distinct problems into one report; tell the user to re-invoke for the second problem

When the task being filed has prerequisites (a phase chain, a "then/after <X>" clause, or a reference to a prerequisite task/goal), depends_on MUST be populated with the prerequisite task ids before submit — a dependent task is NEVER filed standalone

step.title: Preflight: reporting enabled
step.action: Read .tmux-cli/setting.yaml and resolve API enablement: flat `apiEnabled` key wins when present, else nested `api.enabled`.

check.action: Print: "Task reporting is disabled — set api.enabled: true in .tmux-cli/setting.yaml (or run tmux-cli setting) and retry."

step.title: Resolve the problem statement
step.action: If $ARGUMENTS is a path matching .tmux-cli/task-reports/outbox-*.md, read that draft: it already contains the six fields. If it carries an `## Invalid Fields:` line (saved after a backend invalid-input rejection), re-derive exactly the named fields per step 3, drop the marker line, then go to step 5. Otherwise (saved on network/backend unreachability) skip to step 5 and submit it verbatim.
Else set PROBLEM from $ARGUMENTS, or — when empty — from the most recent concrete problem discussed in this conversation.

check.action: Print: "Nothing to report — pass a problem statement: /tmux:task-report <what went wrong>"

step.title: Gather evidence from on-disk artifacts
step.action: Scan PROBLEM and conversation for a goal-\d+ token; if found, set GOAL_ID.

check.action: Read {GOAL_DIR}/signal.json — the verdict, owner, and per-finding detail (failing_command, output_excerpt, expected_state).
Read the highest-numbered {GOAL_DIR}/corrections/cycle-N.md — the last correction the implementer saw.
Read {GOAL_DIR}/runbook.md if present — the ops remedy for a parked goal.
Read the GOAL_ID entry in .tmux-cli/goals.yaml — status, retry counters, failed_by/blocked_by.
Read the tail (last ~50 lines) of .tmux-cli/logs/taskvisor.log filtered to GOAL_ID lines.
Read the tails of .tmux-cli/logs/panes/*{ns}*.log for the goal's namespace if pane logs exist.

step.action: For non-goal problems: collect verbatim error output, failing command, and file:line references already present in the conversation.
step.rule: Evidence is QUOTED, never paraphrased. Cap each excerpt at ~15 lines; prefer the lines that show the failure over surrounding noise.

row.when: planner/spec defect: contradictory or under-specified goal, bad plan, wrong acceptance/validate rules
daemon/dispatch/scheduling: taskvisor tick, dispatch, window lifecycle, budgets, cascade, worktrees
validation infrastructure: validator/investigator behavior, verdict handling, verdict classification
implementer/code: worker behavior, code defects in produced work, execute.xml protocol
anything else: install, setup, docs, ideas, backend itself
blocks goals outright, cascades the DAG, loses work or data, no workaround
degraded behavior or wasted budget with a known workaround

substep.title: title
substep.rule: One line, ≤80 chars, names the specific mechanism — "ClassifyVerdict misroutes broken-gate finding as code-defect", never "validation is broken".

substep.title: description — fixed skeleton
proposed_fix
substep.rule: Concrete remediation at file/function/config level when the cause is known (e.g. "route synthesized error/ops findings through rerunValidationOnly, not handleFailedCycle").

When the cause is unknown, name the smallest concrete diagnostic step (e.g. "add verdict/owner logging to ClassifyVerdict's non-pass paths, then re-trigger") — that is actionable; "investigate" alone is not.

check.condition: derived proposed_fix lowercases/trims to one of: '', 'fix it', 'none', 'n/a', 'na', 'not applicable', 'to be determined', 'tbd'
check.action: Rewrite it — derive from the Evidence section what change or diagnostic would settle the issue. Do NOT submit until this gate passes.

substep.title: expected_green_state
substep.rule: An observable, checkable condition — a command that passes, a log line that appears, a status that changes. Prefer a runnable command in backticks.

substep.title: depends_on — prerequisite edges (REQUIRED when prerequisites exist)
substep.rule: Scan PROBLEM + conversation for prerequisites: an explicit "after/then task <id>", a phase-chain reference, or a named prerequisite goal/task. If any exist, depends_on MUST list their backend task ids — filing a dependent task standalone is forbidden. When none exist, omit depends_on.

Mechanism: pass the depends_on array to the task-report MCP tool at submit, or add edges post-submit with task-link (prerequisite_id per edge). The backend gates claiming on unresolved prerequisites and computes ready.

step.title: Assemble optional payload
step.action: Build a payload map with machine context: goal_id, cycle, window names, key log excerpts — only fields that exist.
step.rule: Payload is bounded: ≤10KB total; truncate excerpts rather than dropping the submission.

step.title: Resolve target project lane
step.action: Call the projects-list MCP tool to enumerate the available project lanes (each lane NAME + its addresses). Read-only; the result names which checkouts exist locally.

AUTO-RESOLVE: if the PROBLEM — or the title/description derived from it in step 3 — names a tmux-cli internal component (daemon, taskvisor, validator, supervisor, plan, investigate, execute, rules, windows, goals), set project=cli. This fires in EVERY context, including autonomous callers, because keyword matching is deterministic and prompt-free.

check.condition: ambiguous (could be the caller's OWN project OR tmux-cli infra) AND interactive reporter use ONLY — invoked directly by a human as /tmux:task-report, NOT reached via the <error-reporting> delegation path or /tmux:task-list consume's cross-project rung, AND interactive prompting is not blocked
check.action: Present the projects-list lanes and ask the user to pick the target lane; set project to the chosen lane. This is the ONLY interactive point in this step.
If the user gives no answer, fall through to DEFAULT (keep the caller's own lane).

step.action: DEFAULT: otherwise keep the caller's own project lane — leave project unset/unchanged, exactly as today's behavior.
step.rule: AUTONOMOUS-SAFETY: autonomous callers (the shared <error-reporting> procedure, /tmux:task-list consume's cross-project filing) traverse keyword-auto-resolve → DEFAULT ONLY; the ambiguous-lane ask branch MUST NOT fire for them. NEVER inject an unconditional AskUserQuestion.
If projects-list returns an error or an empty list, fall back to DEFAULT (caller's own lane); never block submit on lane resolution.

step.title: Submit
step.action: Call the task-report MCP tool with: category, severity, title, description, proposed_fix, expected_green_state, depends_on (when prerequisites were derived in 3.7 — omit otherwise), project (the lane resolved in step 4b — omit to keep the caller's default lane), payload. When depends_on edges are not accepted at submit, add them post-submit with task-link (prerequisite_id per edge).

check.action: The error names the failing field(s). Fix exactly those fields per step 3 and resubmit ONCE. If it fails again, fall through to the outbox branch — and append a final line `## Invalid Fields: {comma-separated field names from the last error}` to the draft, so the retry re-derives them instead of resubmitting a payload the backend already rejected (a verbatim retry of an invalid draft can never succeed).
Print the step-0 enable instruction and STOP.
Write the full composed report (all six fields + payload as a fenced yaml block) to {OUTBOX_DIR}/outbox-{UTC timestamp yyyymmdd-hhmmss}.md (mkdir -p the directory).
Print: "Backend unreachable — report saved to {path}. Retry with: /tmux:task-report {path}"
Print the report summary (category/severity/title) followed by: "Reported: task id={id} status={status}. Track it with /tmux:task-list."

error-reporting.intent: SHARED PROCEDURE — single source of truth. Authored EXACTLY ONCE here; every orchestration command (`execute`, `supervisor`, `plan`, `investigate`, `investigate-worker`, `task-plan-generate`, `project-discovery`, `rules/add`) carries only a short by-name `<error-reporting>` reference into its `<execution-rules>` and follows THIS block. When a referencing command hits a genuine harness/infra/spec defect, it autonomously composes + submits a task-report by following this procedure — no human re-run of `/tmux:task-report` required. Mirror of the `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` shared-procedure pattern in `task-plan-generate.xml`: define the mechanic once, reference it everywhere.
error-reporting.part: Fire ONLY on a genuine harness/infra/spec defect: (a) an MCP tool (e.g. windows-spawn-worker) or a `tmux-cli` command that ACTUALLY errored AFTER its own retries; (b) a contradictory or under-specified goal spec (spec defect); (c) a validator-error-class condition. NEVER fire on EXPECTED work failures — a red validate cycle, S1–S9 / supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, or a test red merely because the code is not yet written. Expected work failures flow through the normal protocol (PUSHBACK/FAILED/NEED_INPUT), not through a report.

To compose and submit, follow THIS file's Step 3 six-field derivation tables for ALL six fields (category, severity, title, description, proposed_fix, expected_green_state) and Step 5 to submit via the task-report MCP tool. Do NOT restate or re-spec the field rules here — the Step 3 tables are the one definition.

In GOAL_MODE the taskvisor daemon ALREADY auto-reports goal-level failure edges (`internal/taskvisor/reporting.go` reportFailure/reportBreakerTrip, `recovery.go` reportWorkerCrash) — so the prompt path reports ONLY harness/infra errors the daemon CANNOT observe and DEFERS every goal-level failure to the daemon (no duplicate report). In standalone/prompt mode (no daemon present) the same harness/infra/spec defects are reported here.
Dedup key = `category + title + goal_id`. Skip submission when an identical report was already sent this run (same key) — one report per distinct defect, never a duplicate.

Recursion guard: NEVER auto-report a failure of `task-report` itself (a submit error follows this file's Step 5 outbox branch, it does not re-enter this procedure), and NEVER report a transient `windows-*` error that was already retried and recovered.

Respect `api.enabled` — inherited from this file's Step 0 (flat `apiEnabled` wins, else nested `api.enabled`). Reporting disabled ⇒ no-op: compose nothing, submit nothing. ADDITIONALLY, this AUTONOMOUS auto-file path (agentic error-reporting composed without a human asking) is gated on `taskvisor.auto_report` (setting.yaml, default false): when `taskvisor.auto_report` is false, this procedure no-ops exactly like a disabled api — compose nothing, submit nothing — EVEN when `api.enabled` is true. This does NOT affect a MANUAL, human-requested `task-report` (this file's Step 0–5 flow), which stays gated ONLY on `api.enabled`.

execution-rules.rule: Local validation mirrors the MCP tool exactly: six non-empty fields, no stub proposed_fix, closed category/severity sets — a wire rejection means this spec was not followed
Evidence verbatim, bounded excerpts, sources named
Only writes allowed: OUTBOX_DIR drafts on submission failure
One problem per report; one resubmit attempt on invalid-input, then outbox WITH the `## Invalid Fields:` marker — never park a known-invalid draft for verbatim retry
